tasks.title: Analyze ClaudeCode competitor
tasks.description: Read the source code located at ../claude-code-sourcemap. Identify its core features, unique capabilities, UI/UX patterns, and any gaps compared to our product. Summarize findings in a concise bullet list highlighting features that could be adopted or improved.

tasks.title: Analyze Copilot-CLI competitor
tasks.description: Locate and examine the Copilot-CLI codebase (assume it is accessible in the repository). Document its main functionalities, command-line workflow, integration points, and any standout features not present in our product. Provide a bullet‑point summary of actionable insights.

tasks.title: Analyze OpenCode competitor
tasks.description: Read the source code located at ../opencode. Extract its key features, plugin architecture, collaboration tools, and performance characteristics. Note any innovative ideas that could inform our roadmap. Return a bullet‑point summary.

tasks.title: Analyze Roocode competitor
tasks.description: Identify the Roocode source (assume it is under ../roocode). Review its feature set, language support, and user experience. Produce a concise list of strengths and missing capabilities relative to our product.

tasks.title: Create SWARMOUT2.md with feature suggestions and implementation plan
tasks.description:
Combine the summaries from s1‑s4 to propose new features for our product. For each suggested feature, define detailed Milestones and tasks suitable for implementation (including descriptions, acceptance criteria, and rough effort estimates). Write the document as SWARMOUT2.md and place it in target/temp/swarmout. Ensure the file includes sections: Competitor Summaries, Feature Additions, Milestones, and Tasks.

The Milestones should be ordered logically (e.g., Research, Prototype, Development, Testing, Release). Each task should be atomic and clearly actionable.

Finally, create any necessary placeholder files or directories in target/temp/swarmout so that the output path exists.
